$comment: Maps runtime dependencies to their codebase context for /app:runtime-upgrade. One entry per runtime SDK: where the integration lives, what confines it, where changelogs come from, which SDK exports we touch, and package-specific upgrade judgment (upgrade_notes).

upgrade_notes: SDK 0.2.113+ ships a native binary and no longer bundles cli.js: CLI path resolution (sdk-utils.ts) and onboarding assumptions are version-sensitive; verify resolveClaudeCliPath behavior on any bump crossing that line.

changelog_sources.note: Authoritative version list and publish timestamps via .time field
Primary source for per-version release notes (87+ releases, some gaps)
Most complete single doc (100+ versions); use for gap fill when GitHub releases missing

upgrade_notes: Versions track the Codex CLI release train and an active `alpha` dist-tag exists alongside `latest`; a fix may ship in alpha weeks before stable (the 0.143.0 logs_2.sqlite fix did). Check dist-tags when resolving the target, and never pin an alpha without an explicit user decision.

Follow the pinned-SDK bump checklist in contributing/adding-a-runtime.md (section: Bumping a pinned SDK): dist-tag check, .d.ts diff of the ThreadEvent/ThreadItem unions and imported item types, event-mapper exhaustiveness compile, runtime conformance suite, one live smoke turn.
The event mapper's `never` exhaustiveness checks fail compilation when the SDK adds union members; that is the intended upgrade tripwire, not an obstacle to suppress.

changelog_sources.note: Authoritative version list, publish timestamps (.time), and the latest/alpha dist-tags
Release tags are prefixed `rust-v` (e.g. rust-v0.142.5): strip the prefix when matching npm versions. Releases cover the whole Codex CLI, not just the SDK; filter notes for SDK/TypeScript relevance.

upgrade_notes: The SDK is only the HTTP client. The `opencode serve` sidecar binary ships as a SEPARATE package (`opencode-ai`, resolved via runtimes.opencode.binaryPath or PATH) and version-couples with the client: assess every SDK bump against the sidecar versions users actually run, and state the supported binary range in the upgrade spec.
npm dist-tags include dozens of `0.0.0-snapshot-*` channels; `latest` is the only meaningful channel: never resolve a target from any other tag.
Server-driven contract: most behavior lives in the sidecar, so release notes about the server can matter more than client-visible API changes even when no imported type changed.

changelog_sources.note: Authoritative version list and publish timestamps; ignore every dist-tag except latest
The only release-notes source (the repo ships no CHANGELOG.md). Monorepo covers TUI/desktop/server/SDK; filter for server + SDK relevance.
